name: From Castilblanco
name_original: De Castilblanco a las Vistas Palacio Cijara — circular
description: Hike a moderate 5.3-mile (8.6 km) circular route near Castilblanco, enjoying views of Palacio de Cíjara and the Cíjara reservoir.
region_intro: Hiking around Castilblanco offers diverse landscapes at the foothills of the Sierra Morena, bordering the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. The region features holm oak groves known as dehesas, numerous streams, and varied terrain suitable for outdoor activities. Natural features include expansive wild rhododendrons and a mix of lush vegetation. Best hiking trails around Castilblanco The most popular hiking route is Castillo de Herrera del Duque from Herrera del Duque – round trip via Sierra del Chamorro, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an elevation gain of 935 feet (285 m). Another top favourite among local hikers is From Castilblanco, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.6 km) path. This trail offers an elevation gain of 478 feet (146 m) and can be completed in about 2 hours 22 minutes. Local hikers also love the Dolmen Valdecaballeros by the Cerro de la Barca — round trip by the Cañada Real Leonesa Oriental, a 10.2 miles (16.4 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 19 minutes. Hiking around Castilblanco is defined by dehesas, streams, and the foothills of the Sierra Morena. The network offers options for different ability levels, with all 3 featured routes being moderate. The routes in Castilblanco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 16 hikers have used komoot to explore Castilblanco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Castilblanco?
A: Castilblanco offers a selection of hiking trails, with five routes detailed in this guide. These routes provide diverse experiences, from moderate walks to more challenging expeditions, exploring the foothills of the Sierra Morena and the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while hiking near Castilblanco?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, featuring expansive dehesas (holm oak groves), numerous streams, and the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Morena and the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. You might encounter wild rhododendrons and various wildlife. Notable viewpoints include the Cerro del Calvario and the View of the Dehesa Valley. Additionally, you can explore areas around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garcia de Sola Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cíjara Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Castilblanco?
A: While all routes in this guide are rated moderate or difficult, the area generally offers a range of trails. For families, consider shorter sections of the more moderate routes, focusing on the picturesque dehesas and streams. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it suits your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Castilblanco?
A: Many trails in natural parks and rural areas like Castilblanco are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. The region's dehesas and streams offer pleasant environments for walks with pets. Always ensure you carry water for your dog and clean up after them. Check local regulations for specific areas within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Castilblanco?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for hiking in Castilblanco. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes, especially the dehesas and park areas, are at their most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are circular options. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101999807/castillo-de-herrera-del-duque-from-herrera-del-duque-round-trip-via-sierra-del-chamorro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Herrera del Duque from Herrera del Duque – round trip via Sierra del Chamorro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1267922340/dolmen-valdecaballeros-by-the-cerro-de-la-barca-round-trip-by-the-canada-real-leonesa-oriental" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen Valdecaballeros by the Cerro de la Barca — round trip by the Cañada Real Leonesa Oriental&lt;/a&gt;, which also offers a round trip experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trails?
A: The terrain around Castilblanco is varied, ranging from gentle paths through holm oak dehesas to more undulating sections in the foothills of the Sierra Morena. You'll encounter paths alongside streams and potentially some rocky or uneven ground, especially in higher elevation areas within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. All routes in this guide are rated moderate or difficult.

Q: Are there any historical routes or significant paths in the area?
A: Yes, Castilblanco is an important stop on the historic Ruta Vía de la Plata (Silver Route), which follows an ancient Roman road. This route also forms part of the Way of Saint James from Seville, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical significance for hikers and pilgrims.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Castilblanco?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the dehesas, and the opportunity to explore the natural beauty of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the hikes?
A: Yes, the region is known for natural features like the El Chorro Waterfall, which can be found near the popular Cerro del Calvario viewpoint within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. Some trails may lead you past or close to this scenic spot.

Q: What are some longer, more challenging hikes in the area?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1266866436/herrera-del-duque-castle-and-sierra-de-la-ubria-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera del Duque Castle and Sierra de la Ubría Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route covering over 24 km with significant elevation gain. Another longer option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1267922340/dolmen-valdecaballeros-by-the-cerro-de-la-barca-round-trip-by-the-canada-real-leonesa-oriental" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen Valdecaballeros by the Cerro de la Barca — round trip by the Cañada Real Leonesa Oriental&lt;/a&gt;, which extends over 16 km.

Q: Are there places to eat or find amenities near the trails?
A: Castilblanco itself offers amenities for visitors, including places to eat and stay. While specific cafes directly on trails are not highlighted, the town center of Castilblanco and nearby settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5667292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera del Duque Town Center&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Helechosa de los Montes&lt;/a&gt; provide options for refreshments and supplies before or after your hike.
tips:
The Cijara Palace is a palace located on the south bank of the Guadiana River, where it forms the border between the municipalities of Castilblanco and Herrera del Duque. It owes its name to the proximity of the Cíjara reservoir, although the land on which it is located is already part of the García de Sola reservoir.
https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palacio_de_Cijara

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This circular hike, covering approximately 8.57 kilometers (5.3 miles), generally takes around 2 hours and 22 minutes to complete at a moderate pace. Your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, despite being classified as 'moderate', the trail features easily accessible paths and a manageable elevation gain of about 145 meters. It's often described as suitable for 'all levels', making it a good option for beginners and families looking for a pleasant outdoor experience.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You can expect to traverse wide pastures known as 'dehesas' and cross several charming stone bridges. The route offers beautiful views of the Guadiana river and the García de Sola reservoir, set within a Mediterranean landscape characterized by oak and olive trees.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: A significant highlight is the opportunity to enjoy great views of the Palacio de Cíjara and Reservoirs. The trail's name itself, 'Vistas Palacio Cijara', refers to these stunning vistas of the palace and the expansive waters of the Cíjara and García de Sola reservoirs.

faqs.question: Can I spot any wildlife on the trail?
faqs.answer: The region around Castilblanco is rich in diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for deer, fallow deer, various aquatic birds, and even black storks, especially near the reservoirs and in the dehesa areas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, Castilblanco is a small town. You should be able to find suitable parking within the town or near the start of the circular route, which typically begins and ends in the same location.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the hike with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and agricultural areas (dehesas) along the route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is typically during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in Extremadura, making hiking less comfortable. Winter can also be pleasant, though you should check weather conditions for potential rain.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this area?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the Reserva Regional de Caza de Cíjara. Generally, hiking on established trails within such reserves does not require specific permits or entrance fees for individuals, but it's always wise to check local regulations if you plan any activities beyond standard hiking.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Castilblanco is a historic town and will have basic amenities such as cafes and possibly restrooms. It's advisable to use facilities in town before starting your hike, as there are unlikely to be any directly on the trail itself.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of easily accessible paths, making for a comfortable walking experience. You'll encounter wide pastures (dehesas) and cross several stone bridges, providing varied and pleasant footing throughout the circular route.

waypoints.name: View of the Palacio de Cíjara and Reservoirs
